homepage.title: Darren Jones MP
headings: h2: Your Voice in Parliament. Your Champion in Bristol.
homepage.text_excerpt: Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Your Voice in Parliament. Your Champion in Bristol. I’m here to serve you . I can help you with personal issues, usually when this involves speaking to Government departments like the Department for Work and Pensions. We call this casework . I can answer any policy questions you have and listen to your opinions on national or local issues. I love visiting local schools, community groups and events, and campaigning for issues that are important to people in Bristol North West. Ask for help. If you are facing financial hardship with the rising cost of living, you may find this resource useful. If you have a personal problem, usually involving a Government department, I might be able to help. I can ask officials to consider your views and I can get you an answer . I can’t make people do things and I can’t help with everything (I can’t get involved in legal processes or personal disputes, for example). If it’s helpful, you can talk to me in person by booking an appointment at my surgery . Ask for help with a personal problem Ask for help with a personal problem Find support for the Cost of Living Crisis Find support for the Cost of Living Crisis Emergency help: My office isn’t an emergency service and we are unable to help with most urgent issues. If you feel unsafe or in danger, please contact the police or an urgent support service. If you’re seriously ill, injured or your life is at risk, always call 999. For non-emergencies, Avon and Somerset Police: 101. For non-emergencies, NHS advice and support: 111. If you are homeless or are about to become homeless: Call Bristol City Council on 0117 352 6800 (Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 6pm. Closed Wednesday 12pm to 1.30pm). Or call the Emergency Duty Team on 01454 615165 if you need help after 5:00 pm on week days, at weekends or on public holidays. Call St Mungo’s Outreach Team on 0117 407 0330 (Monday to Friday, 9:00am to 5:00pm) if you are sleeping rough and need somewhere to stay. Or email bristolspot@mungos.org or contact them via StreetLink . If you or a friend or relative are experiencing a mental health crisis: NHS crisis mental health support: 111 (Available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Select mental health option number 2.) The Samaritans: 116 123 (Available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.) If you are concerned about the wellbeing of an adult: Call Care Direct on 0117 922 2700 (Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5:00pm) if you are concerned about the wellbeing of an adult. If you are concerned about the wellbeing of a child: Call First Response on 0117 903 6444 if you are concerned about the wellbeing of a child. When the offices are closed, call the Emergency Duty Team on 01454 615 165 or 999 if the child is in immediate danger. If you are experiencing domestic abuse: Call Next Link on 0800 4700 280 (Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5.30pm and 9.30am to 1:00pm on Saturday). Call the National Domestic Violence Helpline on 0808 2000 247 (24 hours a day, 7 days a week). For confidential advice for a range of issues: Call Bristol Citizens Advice on 0808 278 7957. Ask a question. You can search my policy database to find out what I’ve said in the past or, if you can’t find the answer, ask me a question . Or you can check the official record, called Hansard , to see what I’ve said and how I’ve voted in Parliament. Ask a question Ask a question Darren Jones MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk | 0117 959 6545 Website made with Squarespace . Read the Privacy Policy . pages.title: Ask for help — Darren Jones MP
pages.text_excerpt: Ask for help — Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Ask for help. Ask for help. Get an answer. We can help you get an answer from a Department, such as the Department for Work and Pensions, Bristol City Council or from a Minister. Support your case. We can sometimes provide a letter of support for your case, application or complaint. We normally can’t help you complete your paperwork, though. Provide advice. We can offer our advice on what to do next, if you’ve got stuck trying to solve a problem. If helpful, you can book an appointment to speak to Darren. Escalate an issue. We can sometimes ask officials to look at your case and ask for updates on when this will be done. Sign your complaint. We sometimes have to approve your complaint before you’re able to submit it, usually for an ombudsman. Collective action. We can sometimes support a group of residents, for example during a planning permission decision. Ask for help. Our casework service is provided by three part-time members of staff. We’re a small team but we work really hard to help you. We will confirm your ask for help normally within two working days. You will then be allocated to one of our team. We aim to get your case started within three to four weeks. Remember, we can only help residents who live in Bristol North West. If you are contacting us about a passport application, please read this guidance first. If you are facing financial hardship with the rising cost of living, you may find this resource useful. Darren Jones MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk | 0117 959 6545 Website made with Squarespace . Read the Privacy Policy . This website has been designed to be accessible but there’s always more we can do to improve. Please contact us if you have feedback on accessibility and inclusivity.

pages.title: Ask a question — Darren Jones MP
pages.text_excerpt: Ask a question — Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Ask a question. I have a small team but we work really hard to answer your questions. If you e-mail us, you’ll normally get a written response within four to six weeks. Mail handling and security checks in the House of Commons can cause substantial delays for letters sent via post. Remember, we can only answer residents who live in Bristol North West. Constituents can also to talk to me about any issues that are important to them at one of my constituency events . (Surgery calls are limited and therefore prioritised for constituents who are receiving casework support to resolve individual problems.) Please note, I’m required to manage conflicts of interest between my role as a constituency MP and a minister. That means that businesses or other organisations seeking to lobby about government policy or funding, whether directly or indirectly via constituents, must be referred to the Cabinet Office . Darren Jones MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk | 0117 959 6545 Website made with Squarespace . Read the Privacy Policy . This website has been designed to be accessible but there’s always more we can do to improve. Please contact us if you have feedback on accessibility and inclusivity.

pages.h1: Open Q&A town hall with Darren Jones MP

Book one of the events below or subscribe to my newsletter to be among the first to hear when a new event is added. Apr 25 Open Q&A town hall with Darren Jones MP Saturday, April 25, 2026 2:00 PM 3:30 PM Google Calendar ICS There’s no set theme for this town hall – come and ask your local MP a question about any local or national issue. Whether you have a burning question on your mind, or you want to come along and hear what other people ask, I'd love to see you at my Open Q&A town hall. The event will be held in the BS11 area, but it is open to everyone in my Bristol North West constituency. Important: This event is only for people who live in Bristol North West. You can check if your address is in the Bristol North West constituency here . For security reasons, the venue details will only be shared with attendees 24 hours before the event. The venue will be in the BS11 area. The venue is wheelchair accessible. Book now > Book now > View Event → Feb 28 Open Q&A town hall with Darren Jones MP Saturday, February 28, 2026 2:00 PM 3:30 PM Google Calendar ICS There’s no set theme for this town hall – come and ask your local MP a question about any local or national issue. Whether you have a burning question that you would like to ask me, or you want to come along and hear what other people ask, I'd love to see you at my Open Q&A town hall. The event will be held in the BS7 area, but it is open to everyone in my Bristol North West constituency. Important: This event is only for people who live in Bristol North West. You can check if your address is in the Bristol North West constituency here . For security reasons, the venue details will only be shared with attendees 24 hours before the event. The venue will be in the BS7 area. The venue is wheelchair accessible. Book now > Book now > View Event → Jan 24 Darren Jones MP in conversation with Ed Winters Saturday, January 24, 2026 3:30 PM 5:00 PM Google Calendar ICS Book now > Book now > Join me for this special constituency event where I’ll be chatting with Ed Winters about veganism and animal welfare. Ed is a best-selling author, vegan educator, and host of The Disclosure Podcast . He has just released his latest book, How To Go (and stay) Vegan. There is much to discuss at this event, including the publication last month of this government’s Animal Welfare Strategy , which sets out our priorities for improving animal welfare, and the steps needed to deliver them. Important: This event is only for people who live in Bristol North West. You can check if your address is in the Bristol North West constituency here . For security reasons, the venue details will only be shared with attendees 24 hours before the event. The venue will be in the BS9 area. The venue is wheelchair accessible. View Event → Dec 13 Winter Social with Darren Jones MP Saturday, December 13, 2025 3:00 PM 4:30 PM Google Calendar ICS There's no agenda or Q&A at this informal event, just some mince pies and and hot drinks (or something stronger if you want to buy a drink at the bar), and the opportunity to chat with me without the stage, microphones or large audience. This will be a smaller event than my town halls to give me the opportunity to move around the room and speak to constituents in smaller groups. I'll need to be a good host and try to chat with everyone, so please don't keep me talking too long! This event is also a great way to meet people in our local community and to chat about local issues with them. Important: Th

pages.title: My Work — Darren Jones MP
headings: h2: My income and office costs:
pages.text_excerpt: My Work — Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Your Voice in Parliament. Your Champion in Bristol. Bristol North West is my home. That makes being the local MP extra special. In 2024, I was proudly re-elected to serve my constituents as the local Labour MP. As your Voice in Parliament, it’s my job to raise your concerns and to campaign for change. As your Champion in Bristol, it’s my job to support you, your family and our community to make Bristol the best place to live and work. My work in Westminster In addition to representing Bristol North West in the House of Commons, I am the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster. That means, in addition to my job as your Member of Parliament, I serve as a government minister and attend cabinet. The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister role is a new appointment, and I’ll be working collaboratively across government to drive forward progress in key policy areas –based within 10 Downing Street and reporting directly to the Prime Minister. The Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster leads the Cabinet Office, including oversight of all policy, national security, and matters pertaining to government ethics. Prior to these roles, I served as Chief Secretary to the Treasury, and before the 2024 General Election, I served in opposition as Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury. I was also previously Chair of the House of Commons Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Committee (which was later renamed the Business and Trade Committee). Darren’s biography. Darren was born at Southmead Hospital in the 1980s and grew up in Lawrence Weston in Bristol North West. He went to Avon Primary and Portway Community schools, now called Nova Primary and Oasis Academy Brightstowe. Darren continued his education at the South Bristol Post-16 Centre at Ashton Gate school before reading human bioscience at Plymouth University, with a focus on immunology. He was elected President of Plymouth Students’ Union, and went on to work for the NHS. Darren later trained as a solicitor, specialising in technology law. He worked in in Plymouth, Bristol and London in both private practice and as an in-house counsel, with a particular focus on the energy and telecommunications sectors. Darren first ran for Parliament in 2010 as a Labour candidate in the Conservative safe seat of Torridge and West Devon. He was selected in 2012 to contest the marginal seat of Bristol North West, moving the Labour Party from third to second place in the 2015 election. Darren was first elected to Parliament as the Member for Bristol North West in the 2017 election and was re-elected in both the 2019 and 2024 general elections. In 2024, Darren was appointed as Chief Secretary to the Treasury by the Prime Minister – a role that he held until he was appointed in 2025 as Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister. Alongside this role, he was also appointed to lead the Cabinet Office as Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster. As a member of the cabinet, he is a privy counsellor. Darren is married to Lucy and has three daughters. My income and office costs: You don’t just vote for your Member of Parliament, you pay for them too. That’s why I’ve always published my income and office costs on my website - so you can see everything with complete transparency. You can check individual costs by clicking the ‘office costs in detail’ button below. pages.title: Contact — Darren Jones MP
headings: h2: Subscribe to my mailing list
pages.text_excerpt: Contact — Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Write to Darren Darren Jones MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Always include your full address when writing to Darren. Please be advised that contacting Darren via email is highly recommended, wherever possible. Mail handling and security checks in the House of Commons can cause substantial delays for letters sent via post to reach Darren. E-mail or call Darren's office Email darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk Please always include your full address in email correspondence. Phone numbers Constituency office: 0117 959 6545 Parliamentary office: 0207 219 5225 Please note the Parliamentary office will not be able to assist with personal issues (casework). If you leave a message on our voicemail, please leave your full name and telephone number. Please note, when a member of Darren’s team returns your call, the phone number will appear as a withheld private number on caller ID. Request a visit from Darren Darren visits schools, resident groups, hospitals, community meetings and local events. These normally happen on a Friday or a Saturday. Please email darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk to request a visit or call my office on 0117 959 6545. Surgery calls are limited and therefore prioritised for constituents who are receiving casework support to resolve individual problems. Please note, Darren is required to manage conflicts of interest between his role as a constituency MP and a minister. That means that businesses or other organisations seeking to lobby Darren about government policy or funding, whether directly or indirectly via constituents, must be referred to the Cabinet Office . Subscribe to my mailing list Please enter your email address in the box below to receive my monthly newsletter and announcements about my constituency events. We respect your privacy and you can unsubscribe at any time. You can read our Privacy Policy by clicking here . Email Address Sign Up Thank you! Darren Jones MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA darren.jones.mp@parliament.uk | 0117 959 6545 Website made with Squarespace . Read the Privacy Policy .
